## THE PROBLEM

Every engineer knows the feeling: you're three days into a codebase you didn't write, and you're still not sure how authentication works. You trace the same call chain six times. You ask two colleagues the same question. You make a change and something breaks that you didn't know was connected.

The codebase isn't wrong. It's opaque. And that opacity costs your team days every month — in onboarding time, in cautious changes, in "let me check with someone who knows this part."

---

### Answers you can verify

| | Benefit | What you get |

---

## HOW IT WORKS

**Step 1: Connect your GitHub repo**
Authorize GitHub and select a repo. RepoBrain clones it and starts indexing — symbol extraction, embedding generation, relation mapping. Medium repos finish in under 10 minutes.

**Step 2: Ask anything**
Type a question in natural language. Three retrieval strategies run in parallel: semantic (what does this mean?), lexical (what words appear?), structural (what calls what?). The best context is assembled for the answer.

**Step 3: Get sourced answers**
Every response includes citations — file paths and line numbers that link directly to your code. Click any citation to open the exact line in the inline viewer. Verify any claim in one click.

## FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

**How is this different from GitHub Copilot Chat or ChatGPT on my code?**
Copilot Chat and ChatGPT have no grounding in your actual repo. They answer from training data — which means they hallucinate file paths, function signatures, and call chains that don't exist in your codebase. RepoBrain indexes your repo first, then retrieves real code before every answer. Every claim cites a line you can open. They can't do that.

**Is my code secure? Does RepoBrain store it?**
RepoBrain clones your repo to index it and stores chunks, symbol graphs, and embeddings in your workspace database. Your source code is not used to train any model, shared with any third party, or retained beyond your workspace. You can delete a repo and all associated data at any time.

**What languages does RepoBrain support?**
RepoBrain uses tree-sitter for symbol extraction, which covers TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, Rust, Ruby, Java, C, C++, and more. Lexical and semantic retrieval work on any text file. Structural retrieval requires tree-sitter grammar support for the language.

**How long does indexing take?**
Most repos finish in under 10 minutes. A 100,000-line TypeScript codebase typically indexes in 6–8 minutes. Larger monorepos can take 20–30 minutes on first index. Subsequent re-indexes are incremental and much faster.

**Is there a free tier?**
Yes. Index one repo for free. The free tier includes Code Conversations, Natural Language Search, Blast Radius, and Living Architecture Diagrams. Pro unlocks all 15 features, multiple repos, and specialist agents (Code Review, Security Audit, ADR Generator).
